title: odontites squarrosus subsp. foliosus
intro: Odontites squarrosus subsp. foliosus, also known as the hairy odontites, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Odontites squarrosus subsp. foliosus include its ability to relieve congestion and soothe sore throats, as well as its potential to help lower fever and alleviate joint pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
